reaction_name: 1,3-Dipolar cycloaddition of phenylacetylene with ethyl nitroacetate to form ethyl 5-phenylisoxazole-3-carboxylate
bond_formed: C–O and C–C bonds in isoxazole ring (C3–C4 and O1–C5)
bond_broken: N–O bond in nitroacetate during nitrile oxide generation; loss of H2O

feed_streams.reasoning: Both substrates are dissolved/mixed in TBAB/EG DES as a single feed. No catalyst or base needed. Pre-mixing is acceptable since the nitrile oxide only forms at elevated temperature, so no premature reaction at room temperature.

reagents.name: ethyl nitroacetate
reagents.notes: Generates nitrile oxide in situ at 120°C via dehydration; 2 equiv used to drive conversion

reagents.name: tetrabutylammonium bromide (TBAB)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent system; acts as reaction medium and may stabilize transition state

reagents.name: ethylene glycol (EG)
reagents.notes: Part of deep eutectic solvent system

mechanism_steps.description: Thermal tautomerization/dehydration of ethyl nitroacetate to generate ethoxycarbonylformonitrile oxide (nitrile oxide intermediate) in situ at 120°C
Regioselective [3+2] cycloaddition of the nitrile oxide (C≡N⁺–O⁻) across the C≡C triple bond of phenylacetylene, forming the isoxazole ring with 5-phenyl regiochemistry

stream_logic.reasoning: Single combined feed: both substrates dissolved in DES. No incompatibility at room temperature since nitrile oxide generation requires 120°C. Single-stream simplifies pumping of viscous DES.

mixing_order_reasoning: Both substrates can be premixed in the DES at room temperature without reaction. The nitrile oxide intermediate only forms at elevated temperature (≥100°C), so premixing is safe and simplifies the flow setup. A single feed stream avoids the challenge of metering two viscous DES streams. The DES viscosity (~30–50 cP at RT) will decrease significantly at 120°C (~5–10 cP), aiding flow.
deoxygenation_reasoning: No oxygen-sensitive species; no radical intermediates; no metal catalyst. Reaction proceeds under air in batch.
quench_reasoning: Reaction reaches full conversion in 15 min. In flow, the product stream simply exits the heated zone and cools. Offline workup (water quench, DCM extraction) handles purification.
wavelength_reasoning: Not a photochemical reaction; purely thermal activation at 120°C.
confidence_notes: High confidence in mechanism (well-established nitrile oxide cycloaddition). The exact viscosity of TBAB/EG DES at operating temperature needs experimental verification for accurate flow rate/pressure drop calculations (Hagen-Poiseuille). The 2.0 equiv of ethyl nitroacetate suggests some decomposition/dimerization of the nitrile oxide — flow may allow reduction to 1.5 equiv with better mixing. Single-stream feed assumption relies on stability of premixed substrates in DES at RT, which is supported by the protocol (reagents added to DES before heating). Superheating strategy (>120°C) under back-pressure is speculative but well-precedented for thermal reactions in flow.

intensification_mandate.flow_justification_basis: Flow provides: (1) Superior heat transfer — rapid heating to 120°C in narrow-bore tubing (0.75–1.0 mm ID) eliminates thermal lag of oil bath, enabling precise temperature control and potential superheating under back-pressure (e.g., 140–150°C) to reduce residence time from 15 min to ~5–7 min. (2) Containment of volatile phenylacetylene (bp 144°C) under back-pressure at 120°C improves safety. (3) Steady-state operation with DES recycling improves atom economy and throughput. (4) Controlled residence time minimizes nitrile oxide dimerization side reaction. Estimated τ reduction from 15 min to 5–8 min (2–3× improvement) at elevated temperature under 5–10 bar back-pressure.
